# AGENTS.md

This file is the authoritative source of architectural and behavioral commitments the
AI agent applies when writing or modifying code in this project. Each block below
states a single directive the agent follows at code-author time.

These are prose-enforcement rules: the agent reads and respects them by judgment, not
by lint or compiler check. The companion file CONVENTIONS.md holds this project's
structured and mechanical conventions, organized by citable rule ID.

---

### No I/O in the wasm frontend
Never add `reqwest`, `fetch`, or any other direct HTTP client call inside `src/components/` or `src/pages/`. All server-reaching operations go through a `#[server]` function in `src/server_fns.rs` (RUST-DIOXUS-9). This is the specific mistake that broke a prior reference app's wasm build — the `wasm32` target's dependency block in `Cargo.toml` intentionally has no HTTP client to make the mistake harder to reach for.

### No speculative database
Do not add a database, an ORM, or migrations because it "seems like the app will probably need one." Persistence is added only when a concrete requirement calls for it, as its own reviewed change — this skeleton stays DB-on-demand by design.

### No speculative auth
Do not add end-user login/session/auth middleware unless the app's requirements explicitly call for it. If asked to build a feature that implies end-user accounts, surface the question ("does this app need end-user logins?") rather than assuming yes or no.

### The design system is the only source of visual decisions
When a page needs a new common UI element (another button style, a modal, a toast), add it to `src/components/` with its styles in `assets/design/components.css`, referencing the tokens in `assets/design/tokens.css`. Do not hardcode a color, spacing value, or radius inline in a page component.

### The auto-capture reporter stays wired
`assets/error-reporter.js` and `src/wasm_bridge.rs` must keep running from first paint (loaded in `index.html`'s `<head>`, before the wasm bundle, and installed before `dioxus::launch` in `src/main.rs`). Do not remove them, gate them behind a feature flag that could ship disabled, or wrap the app in error boundaries that would prevent `window.onerror` / `unhandledrejection` / the panic hook from firing.

### PWA basics stay intact
`assets/manifest.json`, `assets/service-worker.js`, and the PWA meta tags in `index.html` are load-bearing for "installable, works offline for the app shell." Changes to the app shell's routes or top-level assets should update `PRECACHE_ASSETS` in `service-worker.js` accordingly.

### Governance files are load-bearing, not decoration
`CONVENTIONS.md`, this file, and `.github/workflows/ci.yml`'s governance check are the scaffolder's baseline rule selection. Treat a rule change here the same as a code change: state the rule that's changing and why, don't silently drift from it.
